title: Anthropic's Claude Cowork is now available on mobile and web
summary: Anthropic's AI agent, Claude Cowork, expands to mobile and web platforms, allowing background task execution.

title: Meta launches Muse Image, its new generative AI for images, with integration in Instagram and WhatsApp
summary: The Muse Image model allows creating images based on users' photos, which has generated controversy over the use of personal data without explicit consent.

title: China considers imposing restrictions on the export of its advanced artificial intelligence models
summary: Taking example from the United States, China evaluates limiting the export of its best AI systems, which could affect Europe and the global market.

title: Valve releases official Windows drivers for Steam Machine
summary: The drivers allow proper use of the Steam Machine with Windows, facilitating installation and compatibility.

title: The 'red line of death' on Steam Machine has a solution: clear the CMOS
summary: Valve confirms that the red screen problem is not a GPU failure and is easily fixed with a CMOS memory reset.

title: Microsoft cuts costs in artificial intelligence by replacing external models with its own
summary: The company uses its own AI models for Copilot and other services, reducing dependence on OpenAI and Anthropic to save costs.

title: Samsung breaks profit records thanks to its memory chips, but the stock market falls 7%
summary: Samsung's chip division multiplies its profits by 19, surpassing Nvidia as the world's most profitable company, although investors show skepticism.

title: Serious backdoor discovered in Tenda routers allows unauthorized access
summary: Researchers reveal that multiple versions of Tenda firmware contain a hidden authentication backdoor, compromising device security.

title: Sony plans to eliminate physical games on PlayStation and sparks a wave of criticism
summary: After the announcement that new titles will be digital-only, players have started a petition with nearly 200,000 signatures and social media erupts in protests.

title: DeepSeek develops its own artificial intelligence chip to compete with NVIDIA
summary: Chinese company DeepSeek, known for its AI models, is designing a specialized chip that would rival NVIDIA's solutions.

title: X launches a video editor on iOS with subtitle and green screen features
summary: Social network X introduces video editing tools to encourage original content creation and reduce stolen posts.

title: Researchers demonstrate how to trick GitHub AI agents to leak private repositories
summary: A technique called 'GitLost' exploits flaws in GitHub's agent workflows to expose confidential data from private repositories.

title: NVIDIA reveals its new Rigel CPU core for future Rosa processors
summary: NVIDIA adds support for its Rigel core to the GCC compiler, which will be the basis for its future high-performance CPUs.

title: Intel Nova Lake CPUs regain AVX-512 support after its removal in Alder Lake
summary: Intel's upcoming Nova Lake processors will feature native AVX-512 support in their high-performance and efficiency cores, as revealed by Linux kernel patches.

title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.6 'Sol' this Thursday after delay imposed by US government
summary: OpenAI's new version includes the Sol, Terra, and Luna models, and is released to the public after a forced pause due to safety regulations.

title: SambaNova raises $1 billion and reaches a valuation of $11 billion
summary: The AI chip startup secures a new funding round to boost its AI processors.

title: The European Union will require all new cars to include a driver monitoring camera
summary: From now on, every new vehicle sold in the EU must incorporate a driver monitoring system to improve road safety.

title: Hacktivists attack US Army websites in protest against Donald Trump
summary: A group of cyberactivists deface official US Army pages to denounce Trump's policies.

title: 15-year-old GhostLock vulnerability discovered that allows container escape in Linux
summary: The flaw, present in most Linux distributions, allows attackers to gain root access and escape the container.

title: Intel patents new XBM memory architecture that eliminates HBM's silicon interposer
summary: XBM technology stacks DRAM with backside transistors and UCIe links to reduce costs and improve bandwidth in artificial intelligence.
